## Summary

What does this PR change? One paragraph.

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New CLI feature (no spec change)
- [ ] Dependency update
- [ ] Test or CI improvement
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)

## Related issue

Link to the issue this PR addresses, if applicable.

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass locally (`npm test`)
- [ ] If signature, canonicalization, or tier-evaluation paths changed: test vectors regenerated and the harness in `openllmo/llmo.org` passes
- [ ] If the vendored schema changed: schema-drift CI check passes (`scripts/vendor.sh`, `vendor-drift` workflow)
- [ ] Commits are DCO-signed (`git commit -s`)
- [ ] Commit messages follow conventional commit style (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, `docs:`)
- [ ] No em dashes in new or modified content
- [ ] Documentation updated if behavior changed (README, command help, CHANGELOG)

## Test plan

How to verify the change locally.

## Spec implications

Does this PR imply a change to the LLMO specification? If yes, link the LIP that authorizes the change. If no, confirm with one sentence.
